title: Configurable Carrier Transmission Frequencies
description: The system shall support configurable carrier transmission frequencies: DAILY, WEEKLY, BIWEEKLY, MONTHLY, and MANUAL. Frequency is defined per carrier transmission schedule. WEEKLY is the primary demo frequency.
demoDecision: The default demo experience uses manual trigger. Optional Node.js scheduled job is available when enabled. Always-on background processing is out of scope.

title: Manual Transmission Trigger from UI
description: The system shall support manual triggering of a configured carrier transmission from the Integration Command Center UI. The UI shall display pending transaction count, configured schedule details, and a 'Run Transmission Now' action.

title: Optional Scheduled Execution via Node.js Scheduler
description: The system shall support optional automated scheduled execution using a Node.js-based scheduler abstraction (e.g., cron expression 0 18 * * 5 for weekly Friday 6 PM). The scheduler shall be configurable and enable/disable-able without code changes.

title: Manual and Scheduled Triggers Use Same Processing Service
description: Manual UI trigger and scheduled job trigger shall invoke the same carrier transmission processing service. Business logic for batch creation, 834 generation, validation, and carrier submission shall not be duplicated across trigger paths.
architectureNote: Manual Button → Transmission Service ← Scheduled Job. Single shared service prevents logic duplication.

title: Generate Unique Transmission Batch Per Execution
description: The system shall generate a unique transmission batch record for each execution. The batch captures: batch ID, carrier ID, format, schedule ID, trigger type, scheduled run time, actual run time, transaction count, and status.

title: Record Trigger Type: Manual or Scheduled
description: The system shall record the trigger type (MANUAL or SCHEDULED) for each transmission batch. Trigger type shall be visible in the integration audit record and dashboard.

title: Configurable Transmission Day, Time, and Timezone
description: Each carrier transmission schedule shall support configurable day of week, time of day, and timezone.

title: Simulated Time Advancement for Demo
description: The system shall support simulated time advancement for demonstration purposes. Users shall be able to advance the demo clock to the next scheduled transmission time without waiting for real time to pass.

title: Run Scheduled Job Now Without Waiting
description: The system shall support a 'Run Scheduled Job Now' capability for demo purposes. This executes the scheduled transmission immediately without advancing the clock or waiting for the configured schedule time.

title: Transmission Status Lifecycle Tracking
description: The system shall track transmission status through a defined lifecycle: SCHEDULED → TRIGGERED → BATCH_CREATED → FILE_GENERATED → VALIDATING → TRANSMITTING → ACCEPTED | PARTIALLY_ACCEPTED | REJECTED | SYSTEM_ERROR.

title: Aggregate Transaction Responses into Batch-Level Results
description: The system shall aggregate transaction-level carrier responses into a batch-level transmission result: total transactions, accepted count, rejected count, system error count, and batch status.

title: Distinguish Accepted, Rejected, Partially Accepted, and System Error Outcomes
description: The system shall distinguish four batch outcomes: ACCEPTED (all transactions accepted), PARTIALLY_ACCEPTED (some accepted, some rejected or errored), REJECTED (all transactions rejected), SYSTEM_ERROR (carrier unavailable or technical failure). These map directly to integration monitoring dashboard status.

title: Audit Record for Each Transmission Batch
description: The system shall maintain an audit record for each transmission batch including: schedule ID, batch ID, carrier, format, trigger type, scheduled time, actual time, triggered-by actor, transaction count, accepted count, rejected count, error count, and final status.

title: Visibility Into Next Scheduled Transmission
description: The system shall display the next scheduled transmission time, carrier, format, and pending transaction count in the Integration Command Center dashboard.

title: Enable and Disable Transmission Schedules
description: The system shall allow authorized users to enable or disable a configured transmission schedule without deleting the schedule configuration.
